goal: Triage 10 student launch-crashes in 50 minutes with a single diagnostic command each.
thoughts: Pedagogy: give ONE command students paste, they return output, Jordan reads it. Three buckets: driver too old (updatable), GPU too weak (fallback to cloud/pair-up), Vulkan missing (install or unsupported OS). ARM Mac is a no-go — say it directly. Do not recommend bleeding-edge drivers in a managed lab.
code:
# One command every student runs and pastes back:
get_debug_info()                       # GPU, driver, Vulkan, Kit version
hardware_compatibility_check()         # green/yellow/red verdict

# Then bucket their output by error signature:
explain_error(error_text="<DRIVER_TOO_OLD_STRING>")
explain_error(error_text="<VULKAN_NOT_FOUND_STRING>")
explain_error(error_text="<SHADER_COMPILE_CRASH>")

# Optional if Kit launched but console spam:
get_console_errors(last_n=50)

# Verdicts per bucket:
# - Driver too old + lab IT blocks update → pair student with working lab workstation
# - Integrated GPU → cloud session (no local fix)
# - ARM Mac → unsupported; cloud or borrow x86 laptop
# - Vulkan missing on Linux → install mesa-vulkan-drivers / nvidia-vulkan
failure_modes: Telling integrated-GPU student to 'update driver' (will not help)
Recommending bleeding-edge driver that breaks lab's other software
Claiming ARM Mac can run Isaac Sim (it cannot, natively)
No single command — Jordan has to debug each student separately
